Passive/off clause matches CLAUDE.md mode semantics (off => L0 floor only + dynamic verdicts stand down; passive => deny tier, gates stand down) and does NOT weaken active-mode fail-closed - parity-to-Claude binds it.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: A5 (NEW, non-blocking) B3 criterion precision vs redact() for path-embedded secret
criteria_checked.note: Advisory only. Acceptance Criterion 3 promises 'no raw secret content (key/token/.env content OR path)' AND 'matches the evidence-log.sh redact() contract'. But redact() only redacts the command payload; file_path is truncated to 500 chars (evidence-log.sh:73), not redacted - so for a short secret embedded in a file_path the two conjuncts can disagree (parity satisfied, yet raw path-secret within 500 chars survives). Not a fail-open hole: secret-guard.sh's path-only deny blocks secret-PATH reads upstream and CLAUDE.md's instruction rule covers the rest, and this mirrors a pre-existing Claude-side characteristic (parity-honest). SUGGESTED FIX: make the two conjuncts consistent - either scope criterion 3's absolute clause to command/content (and rely on secret-guard path-only deny for the path case), or extend the bound redaction to cover file_path if a path-embedded secret is in scope.
